title: Bakers and flour confectioners
description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders. Mix and bake ingredients to produce breads, rolls, cookies, cakes, pies, pastries, or other baked goods.

tasks.task: Measure or weigh flour or other ingredients to prepare batters, doughs, fillings, or icings, using scales or graduated containers; combine measured ingredients in bowls of mixing, blending, or cooking machinery; and adapt the quantity of ingredients to match the amount of items to be baked.

Set time and speed controls for mixing machines, blending machines, or steam kettles, and set oven temperatures, placing items into hot ovens for baking; observe the color of products being baked, and adjust oven temperatures, humidity, or conveyor speeds accordingly.
Check the quality of raw materials and products to ensure that standards and specifications are met, and identify damaged or expired goods.
Apply glazes, icings, or other toppings to baked goods using spatulas or brushes, and decorate baked goods, such as cakes or pastries.
Prepare or maintain inventory or production records, calculate labor and equipment requirements, and maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records.
Direct or coordinate bakery deliveries and order or receive supplies or equipment.
Conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures, confer with management or subordinates to resolve worker problems, and evaluate employee performance.
Keep records of employees' attendance and hours worked, and recommend or execute personnel actions, such as hirings, evaluations, or promotions.
Enforce safety and sanitation regulations and check equipment to ensure compliance with health and safety standards.
Plan and develop new products and production processes, and recommend or implement measures to improve production methods, equipment performance, product quality, or efficiency.
Set up and adjust machines and equipment, and determine standards, budgets, production goals, and rates based on company policies and workload.

Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, such as inspectors, machine setters, or fabricators; plan and establish work schedules and assignments to meet production goals.
Observe work and monitor gauges, dials, and other indicators to ensure that production standards are met, and inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions.

Read and analyze charts, work orders, production schedules, and other reports to determine production requirements and evaluate current production outputs, and confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations.
Operate slicing or wrapping machines and develop new recipes for baked goods.
